title: Channel Update and September  Book Club Announcement - The Gods of Pegana, by Lord Dunsany
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=CgzCQr9ilsw

A short but historically loaded announcement. Daniel (@dungeondive) floats a question that would reshape the channel: should the book reviews split off into their own channel? The gamers and the readers barely overlap in the comments, and he's leaning toward separation — the poll that (with hindsight) points toward the birth of the book channel, All Fiction is Fantasy. Then the September Book Club pick: Lord Dunsany's The Gods of Pegāna (1905), chosen partly because it's public domain and free to read. Daniel's pitch is affectionate and self-deprecating — he's pronounced 'Dunsany' wrong his whole life and expects to keep doing so — but the case he makes is serious: in well under a hundred pages, Dunsany builds more world than most authors manage in thousands, in King James-inflected prose that reads like scripture from a civilisation that never existed. He shows off a slim illustrated edition (one plate could pass for Sandman art), quotes Lin Carter's judgment that Dunsany's first eight collections contain much of the finest fantasy ever written, and mourns the reviewers whose contempt drove Dunsany from short tales to novels. A one-day read, he promises, and a masterclass in myth-building.

----

This video contains the seed of the channel split — games here, books elsewhere. As a viewer, do you prefer creators who keep everything on one channel, or clean separation by topic?
